recalls.Description: Mad Rabbit is recalling its "RELIEVE" numbing cream, which is packaged in a short, wide-mouthed white container. The front label features the product name in large black letters along with the company's logo and additional details in smaller text. This product poses a serious risk to young children due to its non-compliance with child-resistant packaging standards, as mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. If ingested, the lidocaine in the cream can lead to severe injury or even death.
recalls.Title: Mad Rabbit Recalls "RELIEVE" Lidocaine Numbing Cream Due to Poisoning Risk for Young Children
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to Mad Rabbit via email at product@madrabbit.com or visit their website at https://www.madrabbit.com/pages/product-recalls. They can also access the contact page at https://www.madrabbit.com/pages/contact-us and select "Product Recall".

Images.Caption: Recalled RELIEVE Numbing Cream – Outer Packaging

Retailers.Name: Available at trade shows, tattoo shops, and online at MadRabbit.com from October 2024 through August 2025 for approximately $26.

Distributors.Name: Mad Rabbit Inc., of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging

Remedies.Name: Secure the product out of children's reach and request a free child-resistant lid from Mad Rabbit

recalls.metaDescription: Mad Rabbit recalls "RELIEVE" numbing cream due to poisoning risks for children. Learn how to secure your product safely.

recalls.Description: Kramer Laboratories is recalling Safetussin Max Strength Multi-Symptom Cough, Cold and Flu 24-count caplet blister packs due to safety concerns. These products, labeled with 'Safetussin,' 'Multi-Symptom,' and 'Cough, Cold & Flu,' are packaged in a blue, orange, and red cardboard box featuring a drug facts label on the back. The recall is prompted by the packaging not meeting child-resistant standards, posing a potential poisoning risk to young children if the tablets are accessed.
recalls.Title: Kramer Laboratories Issues Recall for Safetussin Max Strength Cough, Cold and Flu Blister Packs Due to Child Safety Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to Kramer Laboratories at 800-824-4894, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, via email at kramerlabs@emersongroup.com, or visit https://safetussin.com/recall.

Products.Name: Safetussin Max Strength Multi-Symptom Cough, Cold and Flu Blister Packs

Images.Caption: Recalled Safetussin Max Strength Multi-Symptom Cough, Cold and Flu, 24-count caplets

Retailers.Name: Available at HEB, Harris-Teeter, and various regional grocery stores and independent pharmacies across the nation from July 2024 to March 2025 for approximately $11.

Distributors.Name: Kramer Laboratories, of Bridgewater, New Jersey (a subsidiary of Arcadia Consumer Healthcare, of Bridgewater, New Jersey)

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging of acetaminophen-containing medication.

Remedies.Name: Secure the product away from children and contact Kramer Laboratories for return or disposal instructions to obtain a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Kramer Laboratories recalls Safetussin Max Strength Cough, Cold and Flu packs due to child safety risks. Learn more about the recall.

recalls.Description: Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (NFH) has issued a recall for their Iron SAP, Heme Iron SAP, and Prenatal SAP dietary supplements. These products are packaged in white bottles featuring the NFH brand name and a blue leaf logo. The date codes can be found on the bottom of each bottle. The supplements come in various sizes and include specific date codes that identify the recalled items. Consumers are advised to check their bottles against the provided date codes to determine if they are affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Recall of NFH Iron Dietary Supplements Due to Non-Child Resistant Packaging
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach NFH at 866-510-3123 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email info@nfh.ca. Additional details are available on their website at https://nfh.ca/ by clicking on the "Recall" section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Iron SAP Iron Glycinate (120 capsules 1167U)
Recalled Iron SAP Iron Glycinate (60 Capsules 1029U)
Recalled Heme Iron SAP Superior Absorption and bioavailability (60 capsules 1124U)
Please see bottom of bottle for lot and expiration: (Example)

Manufacturers.Name: Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (dba NFH), of Canada

Retailers.Name: Various naturopathic and homeopathic clinics across the nation, A Woman's Time, Doctor Supplement stores, and online at WholescriptsInc.com and Fullscript.com from March 2022 through December 2024, priced between $20 and $95 based on the product and size.

Importers.Name: Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (dba NFH), of Canada

Hazards.Name: The dietary supplements lack child-resistant packaging, which is required by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act (PPPA), increasing the risk of poisoning in young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the recalled bottles out of children's reach and contact NFH for a free replacement child-resistant bottle. NFH is directly notifying all known purchasers about this recall.

recalls.metaDescription: NFH recalls Iron SAP, Heme Iron SAP, and Prenatal SAP supplements due to non-compliance with child-resistant packaging regulations. Check your bottles now.
